But the benefit of this is that there's a clear time Time constraint and this screams to a client that there will be no selling going on here Which puts them in ease, right? Okay, so just understand that this is really something that will develop over years Okay, I'll give you a perfect example with my agency I think I mentioned one of the previous modules that a lot of the e-commerce business owners that we work with now Our deals are structured where Let's say they have a track record of doing 200,000 a month return on ad spend. Okay, so that's their track record You know on average, they're able to you know, someone's will be higher. Someone's will be less And you know, we'll never Taking into consideration black friday or any big sales, but you know on a regular sort of months They're able to accomplish 200k in return on ad spend Well, then in those deals what we'll do is we'll say hey We'll actually won't take a penny until we'll take their average and then we'll give ourselves a 20% margin of safety So, you know from 200k will be down to 160 So we go hey, we actually won't even take a penny performance until we hit you 160k until we pretty much hit your benchmark Of what you've been able to accomplish Now it took me years and years of selling e-commerce businesses to understand that that was a pretty You know, that was an important stipulation and not many agencies are actually I've never heard of an agency doing that Or at least well many people in cpa are but until I started doing it I didn't really know of many agencies then when hey, we're not going to take a penny Right because many people, you know, or a lot more agencies have started to charge performance But you know a smart e-commerce owner, you know, doesn't want to give even three percent return on ad spend on 200k So they're paying you know, they're paying you a six k performance fee just to get the same results that they're already Imaging to get right. So this is stuff where even four years into my journey I started to realize right and this is about a year ago that I was like, okay. I should probably do it where We take a benchmark of what they've already been able to accomplish and we're not going to take a penny of that Right and we're actually going to you know, we will give ourselves a 20% margin of safety So then that way it's such a pure relationship with a client where we have a low base Most of our money comes from Performance and more importantly, we're not taking a penny of performance until we're able to hit the baseline of what they've been able to do
